metadata.rationale: Le socle factuel de l’article est cohérent dans son ensemble : la victoire de Wout van Aert à Paris-Roubaix 2026, l’importance symbolique de ce succès, les références aux incidents de course, à la sécurisation du parcours et à l’écho médiatique du week-end correspondent aux éléments de source résumés. Les images réemployées illustrent de manière compatible les principaux thèmes du texte sans signal de manipulation ni contradiction substantielle.

Since Wikipedia results for the specific query failed, I will use Google to search for definitive records of Wout van Aert's victory history in the Tour of Flanders and Paris-Roubaix to confirm if the claim of his 'first' win was accurate in the case of 2026 (likely 2021 or earlier).
best_trajectory.action: Google[query="Wout van Aert Paris-Roubaix wins history"]

I should try a more specific Google search targeting the 2026 edition specifically to see if there are any records or news articles that confirm or contradict the claim about Van Aert's first Paris-Roubaix victory.

best_trajectory.thought: The claim that Wout van Aert won Paris-Roubaix "for the first time" in 2026 directly contradicts his known victories, specifically the 2018 edition. Given that tool searches for 2026 data have failed (returning no results), this suggests the text describes a future or hallucinated scenario. Considering the prompt asks for an action, I will verify if the image content supports the narrative of the text. The text claims Wout van Aert won a 2026 race. The image shows cyclists, but no clear sign of Wout van Aert winning (blue/yellow jersey leading). The team names in the text are suspicious (specifically "Decathlon-CMA CGM"), which doesn't match standard cycling team names (unless it's a very recent or obscure partnership, but "Team Visma" is well known). The image is likely stock or from a previous year, making it impossible to verify a 2026 event. Therefore, the image does not support the veracity of the text's specific claims.

The text describes "Wout van Aert" winning Paris-Roubaix in 2026 for "Team Visma-Lease a Bike". The image shows a race scene with yellow flowers and spiral road dirt. The prominent cyclist in orange is on a kit with "INEOS" branding (or similar orange scheme). While the general setting is Paris-Roubaix, the specific detail of Van Aert's team in 2026 (Visma) contradicts the visual evidence of the Ineos kit (which Van Aert wore previously, around 2021-2023). Additionally, the text mentions "Decathlon-CMA CGM". The athlete in green *does* appear to have Decathlon branding (e.g. 'Decathlon' text on jersey). If the text is about Van Aert winning and he is on Visma, but the image shows Van Aert "on Ineos" or the "Decathlon-CMA CGM" rider, and not a Visma rider in the center, it's a mismatch. The text says "Van Aert... remporté Paris-Roubaix 2026". If the rider in the image is not Van Aert, or if Van Aert's kit is wrong, it's a mismatch. Since the image shows "INEOS" (orange) and the text says "Visma", and they contradict each other in the context of the future 2026 event, this is a mismatch.
